name: Ted Husing
abstract: Edward Britt Husing (November 27, 1901 – August 10, 1962) was an American sports commentator. He was among the first to lay the groundwork for the structure and pace of modern sports reporting on radio and television.

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Davern
about_quotes.evidence: There used to be Ted Husing’s bandstand... And I liked that. I liked the way those bands sounded.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Davern interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 16, 2001 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Sherman
about_quotes.evidence: I used to listen to Ted Husing on Saturday mornings, the old famous sportscaster.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Sherman interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, June 1, 2006

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Bourgeois
about_quotes.evidence: Ted Husing was more or less our very godfather. He came in next and he liked the band so much that he arranged for us to go on that Saturday night swing session.

about_quotes.speaker: Sonny Greer
about_quotes.evidence: We had three of the best announcers. We had Ted Husing... He wouldn't let us. He would say you play this, and he would pick out some of our finest.
